gaps: A pre-decision snapshot cut (or DECIDED_BEFORE clock) for cells whose docket already contains the outcome — I had to self-censor the disposition entries manually
Relist/CVSG and distribution-count cuts in the statpack are empty (unknown bucket only); a held-case / relisted-petition grant rate would have been the single most useful number here
The petition and questions-presented documents were not provisioned (only the BIO); QPs had to be taken from the BIO's restatement
fedcourts query has no free-text/topic match for SCOTUS dockets (citation lookup on 'Allen v. Milligan' returned nothing), so finding doctrinally similar priors is hit-or-miss
helpful: metrics/statpack.md modern-cert base rates as the anchor
fedcourts query priors showing the multi-distribution-to-grant pattern
Snapshot's full proceedings list (hold pattern, expedite/stay posture were all visible)
notes: Corpus query is expensive on the ranged backend (~140 MB transferred across two calls for five usable rows); a smaller index for docket-level priors would cut that substantially.
